criteria.label: Explain next-token prediction in one or two sentences without calling Claude a database.
criteria.example: Claude scores likely next pieces of text given everything so far and writes the reply piece by piece from learned patterns.

criteria.label: Define token and context window and give one practical consequence for users.
criteria.example: Tokens are subword chunks; the context window caps how much prompt+reply fits—so a 200-page paste may need splitting.

criteria.label: Contrast pretraining at a high level with what happens during your chat session.
criteria.example: Pretraining learns weights from huge text once; my chat runs inference on fixed weights unless I switch model version.

criteria.label: Name one misconception about lookup or learning and correct it with a scenario.
criteria.example: Misconception: it stores my doc forever. Reality: it only uses tokens I send in this request unless memory features save allowed notes.
